headline: Costco Just Hit $1,000. Is This the Year to ‘Sell in May’ Even on the Safest Stocks?
summary: Costco (NASDAQ: COST) closed at $1,008.79 a share on May 8, 2026, leaving the warehouse giant perched just above the psychologically loaded $1,000 line. The setup is what makes the “Sell in May and Go Away” adage worth a fresh look on a name most investors think of as bulletproof. The Setup That Triggers the ... Costco Just Hit $1,000. Is This the Year to ‘Sell in May’ Even on the Safest Stocks?

headline: Why Costco's E-commerce Gains Could Drive Higher Valuation
summary: COST's digitally enabled comps rise 18.8% in April, far topping overall gains, an online ramp-up that could bolster investor confidence and valuation.

headline: Costco's April Sales Growth Highlights Strength in Membership Model
summary: COST posts 11.6% April comps and 13% net sales growth as e-commerce surges and value pricing keeps shoppers coming back.

headline: Why Costco Stock Could Be Recession-Proof
summary: This retailer is well positioned to survive higher gas prices.
